name: Politics Career Ladder
summary: Map how careers progress across public service, political offices, lobbying, journalism, and scrutiny roles.
whenToUseIt: Use this when choosing a path into politics or public policy, or when comparing how different roles build judgment and influence.
steps: Choose the lane: elected office, political staff, APS, lobbying, media, or scrutiny.
List the entry role, the mid-level role, and the senior role.
Identify what the role reads, writes, and gets judged on.
Mark the real career multipliers: writing, judgment, networks, calmness, institutional literacy.
Add the main burnout or integrity risks.
example: A graduate APS officer, a junior adviser, a committee secretariat analyst, and a newsroom producer are all entry points into the same broader field with very different pressures.
